task_completion.description: Navigate to the issues list for the 'Mobile App Beta' project
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser showing the Redmine issues list specifically for the 'Mobile App Beta' project.

task_completion.description: Identify the master issue containing the crash log
task_completion.visual_evidence: An open issue page where the description explicitly contains the text 'NullPointerException' or a Java stack trace.

task_completion.description: Mark the duplicate crash-related issues as 'Rejected'
task_completion.visual_evidence: The other crash-related issues (excluding the master) have their 'Status' field set to 'Rejected'. This can be seen in the issues list or on their individual issue pages.

task_completion.description: Link the duplicate issues to the master issue using the 'Duplicates' relation
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Related issues' section on the master issue or duplicate issue pages shows a 'duplicates' or 'duplicated by' link connecting them.

task_completion.description: Ensure the master issue remains in a 'New' or 'Open' state
task_completion.visual_evidence: The master issue (the one containing 'NullPointerException') shows its 'Status' field as 'New' (or 'Open').

integrity.description: The agent used a web browser to interact with Redmine
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Redmine web interface in a browser (like Firefox or Chromium), not API requests or curl commands in a terminal.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to directly modify the database
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal does not show sqlite3, mysql, or rails console commands being used to update issue statuses or relations directly.

integrity.description: Status updates and relations are made within the actual application UI
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Rejected' status and 'Duplicates' relations are visible in the standard Redmine issue tracking UI, rather than being typed into a text editor or notes app.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Project identifier is 'mobile-app-beta'. The master issue is identifiable by the keyword 'NullPointerException' in its description. Duplicate issues must be marked with the status 'Rejected'. The master issue must remain in the 'New' status. The relation type linking the duplicates to the master must be 'duplicates' (often displayed as 'duplicates' or 'duplicated by' in the UI).
